markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create Playwright/visual-debug tests for rich document creation, code editing, embeds, links, save/load, and conflict display. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: EditorRoundtripVisualTests leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: EditorRoundtripVisualTests is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified dfd7a0f8: rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts Playwright PASS - offline integrated editor + embedded Monaco, data-monaco-mounted, deterministic round-trip, network kill-switch. TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: L25 (suite): 2 uncaught jsdom errors (Range getClientRects) escaping RichTextEditor.test.tsx command dispatch
evidence.fix: setupTests.ts zero-rect polyfills for Range.getClientRects/getBoundingClientRect so ProseMirror scroll-into-view no longer throws uncaught TypeErrors under jsdom
tests: components+editor-lib scoped run: 47 files / 402 tests green, ZERO unhandled errors (was: 'Vitest caught 2 unhandled errors')

evidence.finding: H7 tautological round-trip + H8 zero real-typing coverage + M19 thin visual-lane scope
evidence.fix: harness round-trip now re-hydrates through the REAL editor schema (getSchema -> nodeFromJSON -> check() + structural compare; immediately caught an invalid harness doc - inline hsLinks under doc - that the tautology masked); new rich_editor_typing.spec.ts drives the BUILT editor with page.keyboard: per-keystroke mid-document caret asserts (H1), undo, real-Monaco typing + language-switch hash verified by an independent in-spec FNV (H4), Monaco chord containment (H3), real ClipboardEvent fenced paste (H6), CDP IME composition set+commit (multi-segment candidate flows not emulatable over CDP - documented). Root-caused L11 along the way: codeBlockHash separator was a literal NUL byte rendering as a space; replaced with explicit escape, hashes unchanged.
tests: playwright dependency lane 12/12 green: rich_editor_roundtrip (schemaRehydrated+docEqual now asserted) + rich_editor_typing 6 cases + offline_editor_load + 4 MT-244 specs

adversarial_review_v2.suites_observed: RERUN 2026-06-12: rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts PASS (909ms); vitest app suite 62 files/468 tests PASS with 2 uncaught jsdom errors

findings.title: The "deterministic round-trip" proof is a tautology: JSON deep-clone compared to itself, never re-hydrated through the editor schema
findings.evidence: rich_editor_harness.tsx:109-132 runRoundTrip does serialized = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(state.docJson)) and compares firstCodeBlockAttrs(before) vs (after) - this cannot fail for JSON-safe data. The harness header (line 18) claims "re-load it into a fresh editor, compare" but no fresh editor/schema parse ever happens; schema-level attribute loss (the actual round-trip risk) is invisible. The only real assertion is hash self-consistency.
findings.fix: Rebuild the document through the schema: create a second Editor (or generateJSON/createDocument) from the serialized JSON and compare getJSON() output structurally; better, drive a real edit (page.keyboard.type into Monaco + prose) before the round-trip.

findings.title: No test anywhere types into the integrated editor - the seam hiding the proven caret-teleport defect
findings.evidence: The playwright spec clicks one harness button; vitest tests drive commands/fireEvent, never real typing with caret assertions. The initialContent echo -> setContent loop (RichTextEditor.tsx:156-162 + RichDocumentView.tsx:204-209 + the harness itself, rich_editor_harness.tsx:100-105) teleports the caret to document end on every keystroke for mid-document edits (verified by headless probe against the bundled tiptap 3.13: setContent(identical) moved caret 6 -> 12/13). Every existing test appends at the end or bypasses typing, so the flagship integration defect class is structurally untestable by the current matrix.
findings.fix: Add to the offline playwright lane: click mid-paragraph, page.keyboard.type("X"), assert caret stays adjacent to X and document order is correct; same inside Monaco; plus an undo-after-typing assertion. These are the MT-176 acceptance tests the round-trip spec was meant to be.

findings.title: Claimed coverage scope (create/code-edit/embeds/links/save/load/conflict) is mostly absent from the visual lane
findings.evidence: Inventory ED-TEST-001 names create/code-edit/embeds/links/save/load/conflict; the spec covers boot + selectors + the tautological round-trip only. Save/load/conflict have jsdom coverage (RichDocumentView tests) but no browser-context coverage; code-EDIT (typing in Monaco) and IME/composition have none anywhere. Embeds + find/replace visual coverage is MT-244 lane (skipped here).
findings.fix: Extend the offline lane with: code-block typing + hash update assert; save-mock round-trip through a loopback stub; conflict banner render; viewport matrix per MT-173.

findings.title: Screenshot captured but nothing asserts on it; single default viewport
findings.evidence: rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts:193-196 saves a fullPage screenshot as evidence without pixel/layout assertions or a second viewport.
findings.fix: Add the pixelmatch baseline (devDeps already include pixelmatch/pngjs) for the editor chrome at 2 viewports.

parity_gaps: ED-TEST-001 roundtrip+visual tests: PARTIAL (boot+selectors proven; typing/IME/undo/conflict/visual-matrix absent)
missing_tests: mid-document prose typing caret assertion (real keyboard)
typing inside Monaco updates attrs + hash and survives prose edits
IME composition smoke (CDP Input.imeSetComposition or insertCompositionText)
editor-schema re-hydration round-trip (fresh editor parse, not JSON clone)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-176 requires Playwright/visual-debug tests proving the integrated Tiptap+Monaco editor round-trips content, handles code editing/embeds/links/save-load offline against the real bundled stack with no external app/server. The implementation delivers real schema round-trip re-hydration plus real keyboard-driven browser tests against the BUILT editor, with a network kill-switch and zero-external-request proof. The prior HIGH adversarial findings (tautological round-trip, no real typing) are genuinely fixed in commit 7f8c9f34.
findings: rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts:174-190 — the round-trip is now a REAL schema re-hydration, not the flagged tautology: the harness (rich_editor_harness.tsx:147-185) serializes the live doc, rebuilds it through getSchema(buildHandshakeEditorExtensions()) -> nodeFromJSON -> node.check() -> toJSON(), structurally compares via jsonDeepEquals, and independently recomputes the code-block hash. The spec asserts schemaRehydrated, docEqual, rehydrateError===null, and hash equality. HIGH finding genuinely FIXED.

rich_editor_typing.spec.ts (entire, 524 lines) — real page.keyboard typing against the BUILT editor: mid-document caret-per-keystroke assertion (H1, line 249-269), undo (271-288), real Monaco typing + language switch with hash recomputed by an independent in-spec FNV (H4, 290-327), Monaco column/multi-cursor (329-350), snippet tab-stops (352-396), prose multi-range edits (398-417), chord containment in Monaco (H3, 419-442), real ClipboardEvent fenced paste creating an embedded code block (H6, 444-481), Escape keyboard-trap exit (483-499), CDP IME composition set+commit (501-523). All assertions read the editor's own machine-readable surfaces (__HS_EDITOR_DEBUG__, __RICH_EDITOR_HARNESS__.docJson, data-rt-hash) — no screen-scraping. The second HIGH finding (zero real-typing coverage) is genuinely FIXED.

Offline/native-runtime proof is real: rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts:123-132 installs a network kill-switch aborting any non-loopback request; lines 192-199 assert a real worker-chunk loopback fetch occurred AND externalRequests===[] (no CDN/external editor/server — MT-175 wire-through). tests/dependency_policy/global_setup.ts builds the real dist-harness from source via the app's own vite (no dev server, no mocks); specs require app/dist-harness/harness/rich-editor.html to exist before running.

Resource touch is real Handshake-native: the proof exercises the actual product editor (app/src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx, 61KB; build_editor_extensions.ts; code_block_serialization.ts; bundled monaco-editor assets) — not implementer-authored mocks. code_block_serialization.ts:49-59 codeBlockHash uses an explicit � separator (L11 NUL-byte landmine fixed, hash output unchanged) and the typing spec cross-checks it with an independent FNV implementation.
No deferred-live escapes/placeholders: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/not yet wired/deferred/pending MT-/test.skip/.fixme across the two specs and the harness returned 0 hits.

Scope: the only out-of-app file (tests/dependency_policy/rich_editor_roundtrip.spec.ts) plus the app/ harness + config are within packet allowed_paths (tests, app/src); no src/backend or /.GOV/ product code touched (WAIVER-004). This MT owns no durable PostgreSQL resource — the backend save/load authority round-trip is covered by the sibling RichDocumentView vitest suite per the MT design — so sub_rule_2's PostgreSQL clause is NA for this MT's scope; the resource this MT does own (the real built editor + Monaco + schema) is genuinely exercised at runtime.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet/MT cite v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; the §7.1.1.8 deterministic-round-trip anchor referenced by code_block_serialization.ts resolves under the current bundle. Paperwork staleness only; not a basis to fail.

packet.json (allowed/forbidden paths, DEC-008, WAIVER-004, acceptance criteria PRODUCT-AC-002/003/009)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
